activation_policy: activate when PDF/export, owner preview, handoff, or pre-submission hygiene evidence is requested
completion_gate: S16ExportHandoffPackage proves explicit delivery target, target binding, upstream closure, separated readiness states, successful human-readable export/build, rendered-surface and semantic text checks, manifest hashes, repository hygiene classification, handoff completeness, feedback routing, and owner-gated/no-submission-publication boundary; compiled PDF targets require content-bearing PDF/source-writeback evidence and cannot be template-only or blocked; compiled semantic surface evidence must include body citation anchors, rendered reference entries, visual/formal artifact coverage, internal-lexicon absence, unresolved-risk leakage absence, and semantic-failure attribution routes.
purpose: Export a closed manuscript candidate into human-readable artifacts and prove delivery hygiene without claiming submission/publication readiness.

escalate_to_double_when: dirty-worktree or build hygiene is ambiguous
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Export/handoff is mostly deterministic hygiene; escalate when the output is owner-facing or boundary-sensitive.

worker_packet_coverage.blocker: S16 is a controller-owned deterministic export/hygiene/handoff verifier; verifier subagents may report checklist evidence but fake worker task packets are forbidden.

stage_quality_contract.active_profile_source: explicit delivery_target, closed upstream graph state, repository/build configuration, and owner handoff policy; no global venue/profile default
producer_return_obligations: export handoff package bound to explicit delivery_target and active target policy
upstream_closure_check over S12/S13/S14/S15 plus unresolved/stale node status
readiness_state_separation for content-ready, build-ready, render-clean, repository-clean, handoff-ready, and owner-gated submission states
repository_hygiene_report that classifies intended outputs, source changes, generated artifacts, and unexpected dirty paths
manager_handoff_report, owner_gate_report, human_feedback_intake_route, validator_report, and projection_vs_live_export_boundary

explicit statement that S16 is export/handoff hygiene and not manuscript-quality acceptance, submission, publication, or final scientific readiness; RenderedManuscriptAuditGate is the downstream rendered quality gate

checks: delivery_target is explicit and not downcast from compiled PDF request to template-only handoff
upstream closure and stale/open findings are checked before export handoff
readiness states remain separated and owner-gated submission is not collapsed into handoff readiness
build/render/export/hash/repository hygiene evidence is exact and live/projection mode is declared
human_feedback_intake_route maps feedback to S14/S16/owner by type
S16 does not certify manuscript quality, submission readiness, or publication readiness

downstream_design_force: owner receives a clean export/handoff package with known limitations and feedback routes
S14 receives any export/render/handoff failure with nearest-stage attribution
RenderedManuscriptAuditGate performs downstream rendered manuscript quality audit using S16 export evidence but does not let S16 substitute for quality acceptance

failure_severity_policy.BLOCKING: delivery target, upstream closure, build/render evidence, hash manifest, feedback route, or no-overclaim boundary is missing
failure_severity_policy.MAJOR: template-only handoff is passed as compiled target, readiness states are collapsed, or S16 claims manuscript/submission quality
failure_severity_policy.MINOR: local manifest label, repository hygiene classification, or handoff wording defect without target/readiness drift
failure_severity_policy.WATCH: owner-facing limitation or reviewer risk recorded without forcing unrelated production rerun

stage_quality_contract.nearest_responsible_stage_policy: S16 owns export/handoff hygiene defects; content/repair/rendered-quality defects route to S12/S13/S14/S15 or RenderedManuscriptAuditGate by evidence source.
stage_quality_contract.anti_over_strictness_boundary: MINOR/WATCH handoff hygiene issues can be corrected locally; they do not imply manuscript-quality failure unless rendered/content evidence fails.
